title: Fix frontend data persistence: ensure mutations use authenticated actor and invalidate caches correctly

requirements.summary: Fix frontend mutation hooks to use authenticated actor and properly handle responses with cache invalidation
acceptanceCriteria: The actor used inside every useMutation call is the authenticated actor from useActor, not a default anonymous actor.
Submitting the Student form as admin saves the record and the Student table updates without a manual page refresh.
Submitting the Account form as admin saves the record and the Account table updates without a manual page refresh.
Submitting the Transaction form as admin saves the record and the Transaction table updates without a manual page refresh.
Adding/editing/deleting a Bank Detail as admin persists and the Bank Details table updates without a manual page refresh.
No silent failures — if a backend call returns an error, a user-visible error message is shown.

file_operations.description: Update all mutation hooks (useAddStudent, useUpdateStudent, useDeleteStudent, useAddAccount, useUpdateAccount, useDeleteAccount, useAddTransaction, useAddBankDetail, useUpdateBankDetail, useDeleteBankDetail) to: (1) use the authenticated actor from useActor() inside the mutationFn instead of any default/anonymous actor, (2) await backend responses and check for error variants or thrown errors, (3) call queryClient.invalidateQueries for the relevant cache keys on success (e.g., ['students'] for student mutations, ['accounts'] for account mutations, ['transactions'] and ['transactions', accountNumber] for transaction mutations, ['bankDetails'] for bank detail mutations), and (4) throw or return structured errors so useMutation's onError callback can display them to the user.

Update the StudentForm to display error messages from mutation.error when the backend call fails. Ensure the form uses the corrected useAddStudent and useUpdateStudent hooks from useQueries.ts and shows a visible error toast or inline error message if mutation.isError is true.

Update the AccountForm to display error messages from mutation.error when the backend call fails. Ensure the form uses the corrected useAddAccount and useUpdateAccount hooks from useQueries.ts and shows a visible error toast or inline error message if mutation.isError is true.

Update the TransactionPage to display error messages from the useAddTransaction mutation when the backend call fails. Ensure the transaction form shows a visible error toast or inline error message if mutation.isError is true, and that the transaction history list updates without manual refresh after a successful transaction is added.

Update the BankDetailForm to display error messages from mutation.error when the backend call fails. Ensure the form uses the corrected useAddBankDetail and useUpdateBankDetail hooks from useQueries.ts and shows a visible error toast or inline error message if mutation.isError is true.

requirements.summary: Ensure useActor hook returns an authenticated actor when admin session is active in localStorage
acceptanceCriteria: When admin is logged in (session present in localStorage), the actor returned by useActor is authenticated and can successfully call update methods on the canister.
When no session is present (logged out), an anonymous actor is used for read-only query calls.
Actor identity is refreshed/invalidated when the session changes (login or logout).

file_operations.description: Ensure the useAuth hook correctly stores and retrieves admin session state in localStorage with a consistent key (e.g., 'adminSession'). When the admin logs in via the hardcoded admin/admin flow, set a session object in localStorage that the useActor hook can detect. When the admin logs out, remove the session from localStorage and trigger a re-render so useActor can switch to an anonymous actor.
